subject: DevOps
title: Why Secrets Must Not Live in Code
description: A fast breakdown of Why Secrets Must Not Live in Code for builders.

hook.text: A researcher scanned GitHub and found 100,000 repos with exposed AWS keys. Some were still active.

buildup.text: API keys, passwords, tokens — hardcoding them means anyone with repo access can steal them instantly.

discovery.text: Delete the secret from code? Git history keeps it forever. The old commit is still reachable.

twist.text: Env vars are better but imperfect. They can leak via logs, error pages, or process listings.

climax.text: Secret managers like Vault or AWS Secrets Manager store secrets encrypted and deliver them at runtime.

punchline.text: Never commit secrets. Use a secret manager. Rotate often.

quiz.question: Why is deleting a secret from code not enough to protect it?
options: Git history retains the commit with the secret forever
The secret is automatically backed up to the cloud
